task: Write a Python function second_largest(nums) returning the second largest distinct value in the list, or None when there are fewer than two distinct values. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code handles the case when there are fewer than two distinct values, returning None as expected.
trace: I utilized a strategy to sum only distinct values in the list by looping through the set of unique numbers, ensuring each value is counted only once. By introducing two variables `max1` and `max2`, I stored the largest and second-largest distinct numbers encountered during the iteration respectively. In case of a tie for the second-largest number, the function returns None to adhere to the edge case named in the task description.
